title: Ranthambore Fort
tagline: Ranthambore Fort — heritage city of Rajasthan

overview.short: Ranthambore Fort lies within the Ranthambore National Park, near the city of Sawai Madhopur in Sawai Madhopur district of Rajasthan, India.
overview.description: Ranthambore Fort lies within the Ranthambore National Park, near the city of Sawai Madhopur in Sawai Madhopur district of Rajasthan, India. The park being the former hunting grounds of the Maharajas of Jaipur until the time of India's Independence. It is a formidable fort, having been a focal point of the historical developments of Rajasthan. The fort provides a panoramic view of the surrounding Ranthambore National Park and is now a popular tourist attraction.

topPlaces.name: Siege of Ranthambore (1568)
topPlaces.description: In the siege of Ranthambore, on 8 February 1568, Akbar led a massive Mughal Army of over 50,000 men and besieged Ranthambore Fort. Akbar had become emboldened after his victories at the Battle of Thanesar and the siege of Chittorgarh and only Ranthambore Fort remained unconquered.

topPlaces.name: Shilpgram, Sawai Madhopur
topPlaces.description: The Shilpgram Museum is a craft village, situated near Ramsinghpura village, 9 km from Sawai Madhopur in India. It is a living ethnographic museum depicting the tremendous diversity in crafts, art and culture of various Indian states, especially the western part.

topPlaces.name: Rajiv Gandhi Regional Museum of Natural History
topPlaces.description: The Regional Museum of Natural History, Sawai Madhopur or The Rajiv Gandhi Regional Museum of Natural History, Sawai Madhopur is the country's fourth regional museum of Natural History in Sawai Madhopur, India with exhibits on plants, animals and geology of the Western region of India. It is situated near Ramsinghpura village, 9 km (5.6 mi) from Sawai Madhopur in Rajasthan.

image.alt: Rajiv Gandhi Regional Museum of Natural History

topPlaces.name: Ranthambore National Park
topPlaces.description: Ranthambore National Park is a national park in the Indian state of Rajasthan with an area of 1,334 km2 (515 sq mi). It is bounded to the north by the Banas River and to the south by the Chambal River.

topPlaces.name: Chamatkarji
topPlaces.description: Chamatkarji is an important Jain temple located near Ranthambore Fort in the city of Sawai Madhopur in Rajasthan.

items.note: In the siege of Ranthambore, on 8 February 1568, Akbar led a massive Mughal Army of over 50,000 men and besieged Ranthambore Fort. Akbar had become emboldened after his victories at the Battle of Thanesar and the siege of Chittorgarh and only Ranthambore Fort remained unconquered.

The Shilpgram Museum is a craft village, situated near Ramsinghpura village, 9 km from Sawai Madhopur in India. It is a living ethnographic museum depicting the tremendous diversity in crafts, art and culture of various Indian states, especially the western part.

It is situated near Ramsinghpura village, 9 km (5.6 mi) from Sawai Madhopur in Rajasthan.

Ranthambore National Park is a national park in the Indian state of Rajasthan with an area of 1,334 km2 (515 sq mi). It is bounded to the north by the Banas River and to the south by the Chambal River.
Chamatkarji is an important Jain temple located near Ranthambore Fort in the city of Sawai Madhopur in Rajasthan.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Ranthambore Fort?
faq.a: The best time to visit Ranthambore Fort is Oct – Mar. Summers are 28–44°C and winters are 8–25°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Ranthambore Fort?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Jaipur International Airport (110 km) and the nearest railway station is Kota Junction (111 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Ranthambore Fort?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Ranthambore Fort at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Ranthambore Fort?
faq.a: Stays in Ranthambore Fort start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Ranthambore Fort?
faq.a: The top attractions are Siege of Ranthambore (1568), Shilpgram, Sawai Madhopur, Rajiv Gandhi Regional Museum of Natural History, Ranthambore National Park, Chamatkarji.
